# Questions in Livestream 174

Live Stream #174: The Shroud With The X-Ray Eyes

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=535" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:55</a> | Have any biblical manuscripts been found in the older paleo-Hebrew script? | Yes. He mentions small early pieces and some Dead Sea Scroll usage in paleo-Hebrew. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=673" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:13</a> | When did solar adoration become a thing in Egypt? | He says it appears at least by the early dynastic period, with major cult development by Dynasty 3. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=771" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:51</a> | Is Don Redford's Hyksos-memory explanation for the Exodus a taught Egyptological method? | No. He treats it as Redford's own minority reconstruction rather than standard training. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=1225" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:25</a> | What qualifications did Paul or James have to be apostles besides witnessing the resurrection of Christ? | He says seeing the risen Christ was not enough by itself; apostleship required appointment or selection by Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=1747" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:07</a> | Besides taking numbers literally, what fundamentalist assumptions still cause trouble? | He highlights a wooden literalism that flattens genre, imagery, and idiom. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=2467" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:07</a> | Do you plan to respond to Capturing Christianity's rebuttal of your Shroud video? | Yes. He says a response is coming and stresses that his aim is not to destroy faith but to avoid weak arguments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=2975" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:35</a> | When Genesis says Adam was "with" Eve, does that mean he was right beside her or only that they were both in the garden? | He says Adam seems to be nearby or waiting in the wings, though the wording leaves some interpretive difficulty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=3359" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:59</a> | Is there a way to show that egalitarianism is not sinful without arguing that complementarianism has no biblical warrant? | He says the question should be reversed: the burden is to show that egalitarianism is sinful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=3522" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:42</a> | Did you read about the recently discovered First Temple-period seal amulet from Jerusalem with an Assyrian winged figure? | Yes. He says they plan to cover it, calls it a beautiful seal, and thinks the public description is close but not quite right. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=3592" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:52</a> | What is the difference between a genealogy and a king list? | He says a genealogy can serve many purposes, while a king list mainly tracks succession of leadership. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=3894" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:54</a> | What does it mean for the ground to be cursed in Genesis 3:17? | He says it means the land becomes unproductive: people labor and plant, but the ground does not yield as it should. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=4024" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:04</a> | Does Genesis 3:18 mean there were no thorns and thistles before the fall? | No. He reads the point as cursed agricultural labor, not necessarily the first existence of those plants. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=4335" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:15</a> | Is there monotheism in Tolkien's Middle-earth? | Yes. He describes Tolkien's world as a kind of monarchical monotheism centered on Eru Iluvatar. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=4683" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:03</a> | Where can I read about the priestly ties of baptism? | He points toward Second Temple literature, but says it is not his area of expertise and does not give a firm recommendation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=4815" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:15</a> | What does the Spirit do in communion? | He says the Spirit creates communion and unity as the gathered church remembers and honors Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=5078" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:38</a> | What do you mean when Paul forbids being a drunkard but not simply being drunk? | He treats "drunkard" as habitual, uncontrolled drunkenness rather than every instance of intoxication. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=5435" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:35</a> | Were ancient Egyptians hard drinkers? | Sometimes, especially where wine was available, though he says ordinary beer was usually too weak to be the main problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=5706" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:06</a> | Were slaves in the ancient Near East seen as property? | Yes, though he says ancient slavery could still include side earnings, property ownership, and complicated social arrangements. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=6327" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:27</a> | Is "be blameless" in Genesis 17:1 a command or a condition of walking before God? | He reads it less as a separate command and more as a statement about being complete or whole before God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=6486" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:06</a> | How does Isaiah 6:6-7 work if the Hebrew says the sin is covered rather than forgiven? | He says the Hebrew is about covering, which points to ritual atonement imagery rather than a simple modern gloss of forgiveness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=6833" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:53</a> | What is wrong with Dan McClellan? | He criticizes McClellan as progressive, prone to quote-mining and poisoning the well, and not a reliable teacher. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=6995" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:35</a> | Is there an issue with sola scriptura? | Not if it is understood correctly as Scripture as final authority rather than Scripture as the only source of doctrine. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=7446" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:06</a> | How should we understand the law as mostly case law, as tutor, and as unable to make anyone righteous? | He says the law reveals and teaches sin; it is not a mechanism by which people are justified. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=7934" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:14</a> | How did ancient Egypt keep track of land claims when floods wiped out boundary markers? | He says officials resurveyed after the inundation and reallotted the same quantity of land rather than the exact same plot. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=8046" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:14:06</a> | How do the horses in Zechariah differ from those in Revelation? | He says they seem to serve the same divine-vengeance role, but Revelation gives more detail about what the horsemen do. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=8497" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:37</a> | If a pastor asks me to lead an adult Sunday school class and the elders are unwilling, what should I do? | He says someone still needs to teach; if the elders and pastor will not do it, the caller may have been given the task. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=9410" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:50</a> | When does John 1:19-2:12 take place in relation to Jesus' baptism and wilderness temptation? | He places the material after Jesus' baptism, while noting the sequencing across the Gospel accounts is not simple. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=10942" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:22</a> | Do you know a good agent for science-fiction writing? | He does not have one to recommend yet, and says agent selection is personal and tied to representation, coaching, and negotiation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=11108" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:08</a> | Does Paul imply pre-Adamic people in Romans 5:12-14? | He does not take the passage that way; he reads Paul as discussing sin and death from Adam to Moses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=11312" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:32</a> | In Job 35:6, is Elihu saying our sin does not affect God even though sin offends a holy God? | He warns that Elihu is not automatically right, since Job's speakers can say things Scripture reports without endorsing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=11552" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:32</a> | Is taking offense a type of being hurt? | Not necessarily. He distinguishes personal hurt from an affront to justice or holiness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=11782" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:22</a> | Is it wise to use wisdom from Buddhism, Taoism, or Confucianism to articulate Christianity? | He says missionaries have long debated this and that both cautious and permissive answers have real concerns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=11983" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:19:43</a> | What did you think of the discussion between Michael Jones and Dan McClellan? | He says McClellan poisoned the well repeatedly and that Jones handled the exchange well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=12170" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22:50</a> | Have you seen the Doctor Who "Pyramids of Mars" story arc, and how accurate is its Egyptian lore? | Yes. He says it inspired his interest in Egyptology, though the Egyptian lore is highly stylized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=12599" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:59</a> | Can Bible believers accept the documentary hypothesis? | Yes, in some forms. He rejects it because he thinks it is wrong, not because every version is anti-biblical. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=12737" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:17</a> | What is the worst reading that comes out of the documentary hypothesis? | He points to the Ishmaelite/Midianite reading in the Joseph story as an especially poor example. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=13284" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:24</a> | Do you know anything about the remains of the city of Aten near Luxor? | He says it is a large site still under excavation and potentially important for the Amarna-period administrative picture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=13596" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:46:36</a> | In John 14:12-14, is Jesus saying he will give us anything we ask, or is the message for the apostles? | He says the promise is tied to acts of ministry and faith, not to asking for anything whatsoever. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=13945" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:52:25</a> | Is it a sin for Christians to lend money with interest? | He says not inherently, though lending among friends and neighbors can still create practical and moral trouble. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=14072" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:32</a> | Did Jesus know about modern Western inflation? | No, not in modern economic terms; he says even later economists did not fully understand that, though ancient inflation existed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=14120" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:20</a> | Have you considered reviewing Richard Elliott Friedman's Exodus hypothesis? | He is not convinced by Friedman's view and thinks documentary-hypothesis commitments drive the reading too much. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=14241" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:57:21</a> | What are your thoughts on Rings of Power and Tolkien's philosophy? | He has not watched Rings of Power and describes Tolkien's outlook more as pastoral and anti-industrial than a formal philosophy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=14392" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:59:52</a> | How certain are you that Hammurabi was in Canaan in year 39? | He bases it on Hammurabi's year epithets, especially the year of subduing all enemies, while treating it as an inference. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pjs3dNgZMQA?t=14550" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:02:30</a> | Is Ishtar the same goddess as Isis? | No. He says they have different origins and mythologies, with only superficial similarity. |
